html:
Exploring STEM Museums in Chicago: A Guide for Student Groups

Chicago is more than a city of stunning architecture and cultural landmarks—it’s also home to some of the nation’s most exciting STEM-focused museums. For school groups, Chicago offers countless opportunities to connect classroom learning with hands-on discovery. A visit to one of these institutions can spark curiosity, inspire future innovators, and provide students with experiences they’ll carry with them for years.

STEM Museums Chicago

Here are some of the best STEM museums in Chicago that make a perfect fit for student trips.

Museum of Science and Industry (MSI)

The Museum of Science and Industry is one of the largest science museums in the world, offering interactive exhibits that bring STEM concepts to life. From exploring a real German submarine (U-505) to simulating tornadoes in the Science Storms exhibit, MSI engages students with exciting, hands-on opportunities. Popular highlights include:

  • Coal Mine Experience: Journey underground to learn about mining technology.

  • YOU! The Experience: An interactive exploration of human biology and health.

  • Colleen Moore’s Fairy Castle: A fascinating combination of art, engineering, and imagination.

MSI is a must-see for schools seeking a comprehensive, engaging science experience.

Adler Planetarium

As America’s first planetarium, the Adler Planetarium inspires awe with its focus on astronomy and space exploration. Students can explore the solar system, step into immersive sky shows, and learn about groundbreaking research.

Highlights include:

  • Sky Shows: State-of-the-art digital projections that transport students into galaxies far beyond Earth.

  • Mission Moon Exhibit: Discover America’s first steps into space exploration.

  • Doane Observatory: A rare chance to see celestial objects with one of the largest public telescopes in the Midwest.

The Adler helps students grasp complex STEM topics through the wonders of space.

Field Museum

Though best known for its cultural and natural history, the Field Museum also offers outstanding STEM-related exhibits. Students can explore paleontology, geology, and biology while marveling at world-famous specimens like Sue the T. rex.

Key educational opportunities include:

  • Evolving Planet Exhibit: A journey through billions of years of Earth’s history.

  • DNA Discovery Center: Hands-on insights into genetic research.

  • Fossil Prep Lab: Watch paleontologists at work on real fossils.

Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum

The Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum focuses on ecology, biology, and conservation. Known for its vibrant Butterfly Haven, the museum emphasizes the intersection of science and environmental stewardship.

Students can engage with:

  • Hands-on exhibits about local ecosystems and wildlife.

  • Conservation programs that highlight the importance of sustainability.

  • Opportunities to study insects, amphibians, and reptiles up close.

This museum is particularly valuable for schools aiming to connect science with environmental education.

Chicago Children’s Museum (STEM-Focused Areas)

While designed for younger audiences, the Chicago Children’s Museum includes several STEM-focused exhibits that emphasize creativity and hands-on problem-solving. Students can experiment with engineering in building workshops or explore science through interactive play.

Chicago’s STEM museums provide powerful opportunities for students to learn beyond the classroom, blending science, technology, engineering, and math with creativity and fun. Whether it’s exploring the depths of the ocean, reaching for the stars, or uncovering the mysteries of DNA, each museum offers something to spark curiosity and inspire future discovery.

At Landmark Educational Tours, we design itineraries that help schools make the most of these opportunities—balancing educational value with engaging experiences.
